exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Track a normal secret to move around later.
exists secret.txt
exec sesam add secret.txt
exists .sesam/objects/secret.txt.sesam

# add must refuse an explicitly named forbidden path (config, git plumbing,
# metadata tree) instead of silently doing nothing.
! exec sesam add sesam.yml
! exec sesam add .gitattributes
! exec sesam add .gitignore

# move must refuse a forbidden destination, otherwise a legal move could
# clobber sesam's own config or git filter routing.
! exec sesam mv secret.txt sesam.yml
! exec sesam mv secret.txt .gitattributes
! exec sesam mv secret.txt .sesam/stolen

# The rejected operations must have changed nothing: the secret is still
# tracked at its original path and sesam.yml is intact.
exec sesam verify
grep 'secret.txt' sesam.yml
! grep 'stolen' sesam.yml

-- secret.txt --
Some content
